channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Xi warns Trump that mishandling Taiwan could spark ‘conflicts’
item.dc:creator: Michael Birnbaum, Isaac Arnsdorf, Rebecca Tan
item.description: China’s leader made clear his top priority is the fate of the contested island and its U.S. military support, a striking move given President Donald Trump’s effort to mend ties and deliver trade deals.

item.title: Latvian government collapses amid dispute over breaches by Ukrainian drones
item.dc:creator: Ellen Francis
item.description: Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned after suspected Ukrainian drones headed for Russia crossed into her country, sparking concerns about its defenses.

item.title: Putin remark on war ‘coming to a close’ points to exhaustion, not peace, analysts say
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina
item.description: The Russian president is facing pressure not only from a stalemate on the battlefield but also from a battered economy that is fueling discontent.

item.title: ‘These are our people.’ Lebanon’s medics carry on through grief, injuries.
item.dc:creator: Guy Peterson, Mohamad El Chamaa
item.description: As Israel and Hezbollah exchange fire, dozens of volunteers eat, sleep and pray at a small hospital, waiting to respond to the next airstrike.

item.title: Rubio is banned from China, but Beijing may have found a way around it
item.dc:creator: Michelle Lee, Lyric Li
item.description: Chinese officials are using a different transliterated character for the secretary of state's name, perhaps to allow him to visit without lifting the 2020 ban.

item.title: China gains major edge on U.S. amid Iran war, intelligence report finds
item.dc:creator: John Hudson
item.description: A confidential assessment, circulating as President Donald Trump begins his highly anticipated trip to Beijing, shows shifts in several key areas of competition.

item.title: Facing headwinds at home, Trump signals eagerness to make deals at China summit
item.dc:creator: Michael Birnbaum, Isaac Arnsdorf
item.description: Under pressure from Beijing, the president has indicated an openness to rethinking U.S. support for Taiwan, alarming its backers.

item.title: As the Kremlin throttles internet access, Russians find scrappy work-arounds
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina
item.description: As Putin imposes Iran-style controls, citing security concerns, many Russian fear losing their connection to the global internet.

item.title: Hezbollah’s unjammable drones pose new threat to Israel
item.dc:creator: Lior Soroka, Suzan Haidamous, Mohamad El Chamaa
item.description: The cheap weapons have helped the militants rearm despite the loss of a sponsor in Syria and the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.
